value_accrual_summary: ADA stakers (delegators and stake pool operators) capture block rewards funded primarily by a fixed reserve-emission schedule (~0.3% of remaining reserve drawn each epoch), with transaction fees playing a minor supplementary role; there is no token burn mechanism, so value accrual depends entirely on demand-side appreciation rather than supply reduction.

_notes.burn: Cardano has no burn mechanism. The protocol does not destroy ADA from fees or any other source. Unclaimed rewards expire and return to the reserve, but this is not a burn.
_notes.supply_cap: Hard cap of 45 billion ADA. As of mid-2025, ~35.8B ADA is in circulation; ~8B ADA remains in the reserve.
_notes.emission_mechanics: Each epoch (~5 days), 0.3% of the remaining reserve is released into a 'virtual pot'. 20% of that pot goes to the treasury; 80% is distributed to stakers and SPOs, supplemented by transaction fees collected that epoch. Because reserve emissions dwarf fee revenue, yield is predominantly inflationary.
_notes.fee_revenue_context: Transaction fees are real but small relative to reserve emissions. Fees flow into the epoch reward pot alongside reserve releases — they do not accrue to a protocol treasury or get burned.
_notes.staking_apr: ~3% APY is the current network average per StakingRewards and Cardano Explorer data; it declines over time as the reserve depletes.
_notes.governance: CIP-1694 (Voltaire era) introduces on-chain governance but does not introduce a burn or fee-buyback mechanism as of the research date.
